content.thinking: The user wants me to create a .md file listing all the skills, GSD commands, superpowers, and agents available. Let me compile this information from the system reminder and my tool descriptions, then write it to the User folder.

| `springboot-patterns` | Architecture Spring Boot, REST API, services |
| `springboot-security` | Spring Security best practices |

| `clickhouse-io` | ClickHouse : patterns BDD, optimisation queries, analytics |

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `test` | Assistant de tests |
| `test-driven-development` | TDD : ecrire les tests avant l'implementation |
| `tdd-workflow` | Workflow TDD pour features, bugfix, refactoring |

| `qa-expert` | Processus de QA testing complet |
| `senior-qa` | Generation de tests, tests unitaires, analyse |
| `coding-standards` | Standards de code universels : TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Python, Go |
| `code-reviewer` | Revue de code automatisee pour TS, JS, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin |
| `verification-loop` | Systeme de verification complet pour sessions Claude Code |
| `verification-before-completion` | Verification avant de declarer un travail termine |
| `eval-harness` | Framework d'evaluation formel pour sessions Claude Code |

### Securite

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `security-review` | Review securite pour auth, user input, secrets, creation |

|-------|-------------|

### Design & UI

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `algorithmic-art` | Art algorithmique avec p5.js, randomness seedee, parametres interactifs |
| `canvas-design` | Art visuel en .png et .pdf avec philosophie design |

| `ui-ux-pro-max` | Design UI/UX : 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 font pairings, 25 charts |
| `web-artifacts-builder` | Creation d'artifacts HTML multi-composants |
| `slack-gif-creator` | Creation de GIFs animes optimises pour Slack |

### Productivite & Workflow

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `brainstorming` | A utiliser avant tout travail creatif |
| `deep-research` | Rapports de recherche avec citations et suivi des preuves |
| `fact-checker` | Verification de faits via recherche web et sources officielles |
| `prompt-optimizer` | Transformation de prompts vagues en specifications precises (EARS) |
| `senior-prompt-engineer` | Optimisation de prompts, design de templates |
| `doc-coauthoring` | Co-redaction de documentation structuree |
| `writing-plans` | Planification avant le code quand il y a des specs/requirements |
| `executing-plans` | Execution de plans d'implementation avec review |
| `search-first` | Recherche avant codage : trouver les outils existants d'abord |
| `strategic-compact` | Compaction manuelle du contexte a intervalles logiques |
| `iterative-retrieval` | Raffinement progressif de la recuperation de contexte |

### Git & GitHub

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `github-ops` | Operations GitHub completes via gh CLI et API |
| `using-git-worktrees` | Travail isole avec git worktrees |
| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Finalisation d'une branche de dev : merge, integration |
| `requesting-code-review` | Demande de code review apres completion |
| `receiving-code-review` | Reception et implementation de feedback de review |

### MCP & Skills

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `mcp-builder` | Guide pour creer des serveurs MCP de qualite |
| `skill-creator` | Guide pour creer des skills efficaces |
| `agent-skill-creator` | Creation d'agents et automatisation de taches repetitives |
| `skill-reviewer` | Review et amelioration de skills Claude Code |
| `skills-search` | Recherche, decouverte, installation de skills |
| `writing-skills` | Creation, edition, verification de skills |
| `configure-ecc` | Installateur interactif Everything Claude Code |

### Internationalisation & Communication

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `i18n-expert` | Internationalisation et localisation |
| `internal-comms` | Communications internes avec formats adaptes |
| `scrum-master` | Scrum Master avance : analyse sante equipe, velocity, retros |
| `senior-pm` | Chef de projet senior : logiciel enterprise, SaaS, transformation digitale |

### Patterns avances

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `content-hash-cache-pattern` | Cache par hash SHA-256 du contenu des fichiers |
| `cost-aware-llm-pipeline` | Optimisation de couts pour usage API LLM |
| `regex-vs-llm-structured-text` | Framework de decision regex vs LLM pour parsing de texte |
| `continuous-learning` | Extraction de patterns reutilisables depuis les sessions |
| `continuous-learning-v2` | Systeme d'apprentissage par instincts via hooks |

### Utilitaires rapides

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `lint` | Assistant Linting |
| `debug` | Assistant Debug |
| `refactor` | Assistant Refactoring |
| `npm-scripts` | Assistant NPM Scripts |
| `ultra-think` | Analyse profonde et resolution de problemes multi-dimensionnelle |
| `keybindings-help` | Personnalisation des raccourcis clavier |
| `claude-developer-platform` | Programmes utilisant l'API Claude / Anthropic |
| `using-superpowers` | Etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallelisation de taches independantes |
| `subagent-driven-development` | Execution de plans avec taches independantes en session |
| `systematic-debugging` | Debugging systematique avant de proposer des corrections |

---

## GSD (Get Stuff Done) - Commandes

Systeme de gestion de projet structure avec roadmap, phases, milestones et execution.

### Gestion de projet

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:new-project` | Initialiser un nouveau projet avec context gathering et PROJECT.md |
| `/gsd:new-milestone` | Demarrer un nouveau cycle de milestone |
| `/gsd:progress` | Verifier la progression du projet et router vers l'action suivante |
| `/gsd:map-codebase` | Analyser le codebase avec des agents mappers paralleles |
| `/gsd:health` | Diagnostiquer la sante du repertoire de planning |

### Planification

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:plan-phase` | Creer un plan de phase detaille (PLAN.md) avec verification |
| `/gsd:discuss-phase` | Questions adaptatives avant la planification d'une phase |
| `/gsd:list-phase-assumptions` | Reveler les hypotheses de Claude sur l'approche d'une phase |
| `/gsd:research-phase` | Rechercher comment implementer une phase |
| `/gsd:plan-milestone-gaps` | Creer des phases pour combler les lacunes d'un audit milestone |

### Gestion des phases

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-phase` | Ajouter une phase a la fin du milestone courant |
| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |

### Verification & Completion

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |

### Gestion de session

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |

### Taches & Maintenance

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |

### Configuration & Aide

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |

---

## Agents (sous-agents specialises)

Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.

### Agents generaux

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |

### Agents GSD

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |

### Agents utilitaires

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |

---

## Superpowers

Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :

- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes

### Capacites cles

- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees

---

*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*

| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |

### Verification & Completion

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |

### Gestion de session

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |

### Taches & Maintenance

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |

### Configuration & Aide

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |

---

## Agents (sous-agents specialises)

Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.

### Agents generaux

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |

### Agents GSD

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |

### Agents utilitaires

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |

---

## Superpowers

Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :

- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes

### Capacites cles

- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees

---

*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*

### Testing & Qualite

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|

| `test-driven-development` | TDD : ecrire les tests avant l'implementation |
| `tdd-workflow` | Workflow TDD pour features, bugfix, refactoring |
| `e2e-testing` | Tests E2E avec Playwright : Page Object Model, CI/CD |
| `webapp-testing` | Tests d'applications web locales avec Playwright |
| `qa-expert` | Processus de QA testing complet |
| `senior-qa` | Generation de tests, tests unitaires, analyse |
| `coding-standards` | Standards de code universels : TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Python, Go |
| `code-reviewer` | Revue de code automatisee pour TS, JS, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin |
| `verification-loop` | Systeme de verification complet pour sessions Claude Code |
| `verification-before-completion` | Verification avant de declarer un travail termine |

| `security-scan` | Scan securite de la configuration Claude Code (.claude/) |
| `senior-security` | Threat modeling, analyse vulnerabilites, architecture securisee |
| `owasp-security` | OWASP Top 10 2025, ASVS 5.0, Agentic AI security 2026 |

### DevOps & Deployment

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|

| `deployment-patterns` | Workflows de deploiement, CI/CD, Docker, health checks, rollback |

### Documents & Media

### Design & UI

### Utilitaires rapides

| `claude-developer-platform` | Programmes utilisant l'API Claude / Anthropic |
| `using-superpowers` | Etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallelisation de taches independantes |
| `subagent-driven-development` | Execution de plans avec taches independantes en session |
| `systematic-debugging` | Debugging systematique avant de proposer des corrections |

---

## GSD (Get Stuff Done) - Commandes

Systeme de gestion de projet structure avec roadmap, phases, milestones et execution.

### Gestion de projet

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:new-project` | Initialiser un nouveau projet avec context gathering et PROJECT.md |
| `/gsd:new-milestone` | Demarrer un nouveau cycle de milestone |
| `/gsd:progress` | Verifier la progression du projet et router vers l'action suivante |
| `/gsd:map-codebase` | Analyser le codebase avec des agents mappers paralleles |
| `/gsd:health` | Diagnostiquer la sante du repertoire de planning |

### Planification

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:plan-phase` | Creer un plan de phase detaille (PLAN.md) avec verification |
| `/gsd:discuss-phase` | Questions adaptatives avant la planification d'une phase |
| `/gsd:list-phase-assumptions` | Reveler les hypotheses de Claude sur l'approche d'une phase |
| `/gsd:research-phase` | Rechercher comment implementer une phase |
| `/gsd:plan-milestone-gaps` | Creer des phases pour combler les lacunes d'un audit milestone |

### Gestion des phases

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-phase` | Ajouter une phase a la fin du milestone courant |
| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |

### Verification & Completion

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |

### Gestion de session

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |

### Taches & Maintenance

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |

### Configuration & Aide

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |

---

## Agents (sous-agents specialises)

Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.

### Agents generaux

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |

### Agents GSD

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |

### Agents utilitaires

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |

---

## Superpowers

Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :

- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes

### Capacites cles

- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees

---

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

This is a JavaScript/TypeScript project optimized for modern web development. The project uses industry-standard tools and follows best practices for scalable application development.

## Development Commands

### Package Management
- `npm install` or `yarn install` - Install dependencies
- `npm ci` or `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` - Install dependencies for CI/CD
- `npm update` or `yarn upgrade` - Update dependencies

### Build Commands
- `npm run build` - Build the project for production
- `npm run dev` or `npm start` - Start development server
- `npm run preview` - Preview production build locally

### Testing Commands
- `npm test` or `npm run test` - Run all tests
- `npm run test:watch` - Run tests in watch mode
- `npm run test:coverage` - Run tests with coverage report
- `npm run test:unit` - Run unit tests only
- `npm run test:integration` - Run integration tests only
- `npm run test:e2e` - Run end-to-end tests

### Code Quality Commands
- `npm run lint` - Run ESLint for code linting
- `npm run lint:fix` - Run ESLint with auto-fix
- `npm run format` - Format code with Prettier
- `npm run format:check` - Check code formatting
- `npm run typecheck` - Run TypeScript type checking

### Development Tools
- `npm run storybook` - Start Storybook (if available)
- `npm run analyze` - Analyze bundle size
- `npm run clean` - Clean build artifacts

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies
- **JavaScript/TypeScript** - Primary programming languages
- **Node.js** - Runtime environment
- **npm/yarn** - Package management

### Common Frameworks
- **React** - UI library with hooks and functional components
- **Vue.js** - Progressive framework for building user interfaces
- **Angular** - Full-featured framework for web applications
- **Express.js** - Web application framework for Node.js
- **Next.js** - React framework with SSR/SSG capabilities

### Build Tools
- **Vite** - Fast build tool and development server
- **Webpack** - Module bundler
- **Rollup** - Module bundler for libraries
- **esbuild** - Extremely fast JavaScript bundler

### Testing Framework
- **Jest** - JavaScript testing framework
- **Vitest** - Fast unit test framework
- **Testing Library** - Simple and complete testing utilities
- **Cypress** - End-to-end testing framework
- **Playwright** - Cross-browser testing

### Code Quality Tools
- **ESLint** - JavaScript/TypeScript linter
- **Prettier** - Code formatter
- **TypeScript** - Static type checking
- **Husky** - Git hooks

## Project Structure Guidelines

### File Organization
```
src/
├── components/     # Reusable UI components
├── pages/         # Page components or routes
├── hooks/         # Custom React hooks
├── utils/         # Utility functions
├── services/      # API calls and external services
├── types/         # TypeScript type definitions
├── constants/     # Application constants
├── styles/        # Global styles and themes
└── tests/         # Test files
```

### Naming Conventions
- **Files**: Use kebab-case for file names (`user-profile.component.ts`)
- **Components**: Use PascalCase for component names (`UserProfile`)
- **Functions**: Use camelCase for function names (`getUserData`)
- **Constants**: Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants (`API_BASE_URL`)
- **Types/Interfaces**: Use PascalCase with descriptive names (`UserData`, `ApiResponse`)

## TypeScript Guidelines

### Type Safety
- Enable strict mode in `tsconfig.json`
- Use explicit types for function parameters and return values
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Use union types for multiple possible values
- Avoid `any` type - use `unknown` when type is truly unknown

### Best Practices
- Use type guards for runtime type checking
- Leverage utility types (`Partial`, `Pick`, `Omit`, etc.)
- Create custom types for domain-specific data
- Use enums for finite sets of values
- Document complex types with JSDoc comments

## Code Quality Standards

### ESLint Configuration
- Use recommended ESLint rules for JavaScript/TypeScript
- Enable React-specific rules if using React
- Configure import/export rules for consistent module usage
- Set up accessibility rules for inclusive development

### Prettier Configuration
- Use consistent indentation (2 spaces recommended)
- Set maximum line length (80-100 characters)
- Use single quotes for strings
- Add trailing commas for better git diffs

### Testing Standards
- Aim for 80%+ test coverage
- Write unit tests for utilities and business logic
- Use integration tests for component interactions
- Implement e2e tests for critical user flows
- Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)

## Performance Optimization

### Bundle Optimization
- Use code splitting for large applications
- Implement lazy loading for routes and components
- Optimize images and assets
- Use tree shaking to eliminate dead code
- Analyze bundle size regularly

### Runtime Performance
- Implement proper memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback)
- Use virtualization for large lists
- Optimize re-renders in React applications
- Implement proper error boundaries
- Use web workers for heavy computations

## Security Guidelines

### Dependencies
- Regularly audit dependencies with `npm audit`
- Keep dependencies updated
- Use lock files (`package-lock.json`, `yarn.lock`)
- Avoid dependencies with known vulnerabilities

### Code Security
- Sanitize user inputs
- Use HTTPS for API calls
- Implement proper authentication and authorization
- Store sensitive data securely (environment variables)
- Use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers

## Development Workflow

### Before Starting
1. Check Node.js version compatibility
2. Install dependencies with `npm install`
3. Copy environment variables from `.env.example`
4. Run type checking with `npm run typecheck`

### During Development
1. Use TypeScript for type safety
2. Run linter frequently to catch issues early
3. Write tests for new features
4. Use meaningful commit messages
5. Review code changes before committing

### Before Committing
1. Run full test suite: `npm test`
2. Check linting: `npm run lint`
3. Verify formatting: `npm run format:check`
4. Run type checking: `npm run typecheck`
5. Test production build: `npm run build`

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Directive de session obligatoire

**A CHAQUE NOUVELLE SESSION**, Claude Code DOIT :

1. **Consulter le fichier `~/claude-capabilities.md`** pour connaitre l'ensemble des skills, commandes GSD, agents et superpowers disponibles.
2. **Analyser la demande de l'utilisateur** et identifier quels skills, agents ou commandes GSD sont les plus adaptes pour y repondre.
3. **Utiliser proactivement les skills et agents pertinents** plutot que de tout faire manuellement. Par exemple :
   - Pour du debug → utiliser le skill `systematic-debugging` ou `debug`
   - Pour de l'architecture → utiliser `senior-architect` ou `Plan` agent
   - Pour des tests → utiliser `test`, `tdd-workflow`, ou le framework adapte au langage
   - Pour de la securite → utiliser `security-review` ou `owasp-security`
   - Pour la gestion de projet → utiliser les commandes `/gsd:*`
   - Pour de la recherche → utiliser l'agent `Explore` ou `deep-research`
   - Pour des taches paralleles → utiliser `dispatching-parallel-agents`
4. **Proposer les skills pertinents** a l'utilisateur quand une tache pourrait en beneficier, meme si l'utilisateur ne les mentionne pas explicitement.

> **Fichier de reference** : `C:\Users\User\claude-capabilities.md`
> Ce fichier contient le catalogue complet des ~70 skills, ~25 commandes GSD, ~15 agents et les superpowers.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

This is a JavaScript/TypeScript project optimized for modern web development. The project uses industry-standard tools and follows best practices for scalable application development.

## Development Commands

### Package Management
- `npm install` or `yarn install` - Install dependencies
- `npm ci` or `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` - Install dependencies for CI/CD
- `npm update` or `yarn upgrade` - Update dependencies

### Build Commands
- `npm run build` - Build the project for production
- `npm run dev` or `npm start` - Start development server
- `npm run preview` - Preview production build locally

### Testing Commands
- `npm test` or `npm run test` - Run all tests
- `npm run test:watch` - Run tests in watch mode
- `npm run test:coverage` - Run tests with coverage report
- `npm run test:unit` - Run unit tests only
- `npm run test:integration` - Run integration tests only
- `npm run test:e2e` - Run end-to-end tests

### Code Quality Commands
- `npm run lint` - Run ESLint for code linting
- `npm run lint:fix` - Run ESLint with auto-fix
- `npm run format` - Format code with Prettier
- `npm run format:check` - Check code formatting
- `npm run typecheck` - Run TypeScript type checking

### Development Tools
- `npm run storybook` - Start Storybook (if available)
- `npm run analyze` - Analyze bundle size
- `npm run clean` - Clean build artifacts

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies
- **JavaScript/TypeScript** - Primary programming languages
- **Node.js** - Runtime environment
- **npm/yarn** - Package management

### Common Frameworks
- **React** - UI library with hooks and functional components
- **Vue.js** - Progressive framework for building user interfaces
- **Angular** - Full-featured framework for web applications
- **Express.js** - Web application framework for Node.js
- **Next.js** - React framework with SSR/SSG capabilities

### Build Tools
- **Vite** - Fast build tool and development server
- **Webpack** - Module bundler
- **Rollup** - Module bundler for libraries
- **esbuild** - Extremely fast JavaScript bundler

### Testing Framework
- **Jest** - JavaScript testing framework
- **Vitest** - Fast unit test framework
- **Testing Library** - Simple and complete testing utilities
- **Cypress** - End-to-end testing framework
- **Playwright** - Cross-browser testing

### Code Quality Tools
- **ESLint** - JavaScript/TypeScript linter
- **Prettier** - Code formatter
- **TypeScript** - Static type checking
- **Husky** - Git hooks

## Project Structure Guidelines

### File Organization
```
src/
├── components/     # Reusable UI components
├── pages/         # Page components or routes
├── hooks/         # Custom React hooks
├── utils/         # Utility functions
├── services/      # API calls and external services
├── types/         # TypeScript type definitions
├── constants/     # Application constants
├── styles/        # Global styles and themes
└── tests/         # Test files
```

### Naming Conventions
- **Files**: Use kebab-case for file names (`user-profile.component.ts`)
- **Components**: Use PascalCase for component names (`UserProfile`)
- **Functions**: Use camelCase for function names (`getUserData`)
- **Constants**: Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants (`API_BASE_URL`)
- **Types/Interfaces**: Use PascalCase with descriptive names (`UserData`, `ApiResponse`)

## TypeScript Guidelines

### Type Safety
- Enable strict mode in `tsconfig.json`
- Use explicit types for function parameters and return values
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Use union types for multiple possible values
- Avoid `any` type - use `unknown` when type is truly unknown

### Best Practices
- Use type guards for runtime type checking
- Leverage utility types (`Partial`, `Pick`, `Omit`, etc.)
- Create custom types for domain-specific data
- Use enums for finite sets of values
- Document complex types with JSDoc comments

## Code Quality Standards

### ESLint Configuration
- Use recommended ESLint rules for JavaScript/TypeScript
- Enable React-specific rules if using React
- Configure import/export rules for consistent module usage
- Set up accessibility rules for inclusive development

### Prettier Configuration
- Use consistent indentation (2 spaces recommended)
- Set maximum line length (80-100 characters)
- Use single quotes for strings
- Add trailing commas for better git diffs

### Testing Standards
- Aim for 80%+ test coverage
- Write unit tests for utilities and business logic
- Use integration tests for component interactions
- Implement e2e tests for critical user flows
- Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)

## Performance Optimization

### Bundle Optimization
- Use code splitting for large applications
- Implement lazy loading for routes and components
- Optimize images and assets
- Use tree shaking to eliminate dead code
- Analyze bundle size regularly

### Runtime Performance
- Implement proper memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback)
- Use virtualization for large lists
- Optimize re-renders in React applications
- Implement proper error boundaries
- Use web workers for heavy computations

## Security Guidelines

### Dependencies
- Regularly audit dependencies with `npm audit`
- Keep dependencies updated
- Use lock files (`package-lock.json`, `yarn.lock`)
- Avoid dependencies with known vulnerabilities

### Code Security
- Sanitize user inputs
- Use HTTPS for API calls
- Implement proper authentication and authorization
- Store sensitive data securely (environment variables)
- Use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers

## Development Workflow

### Before Starting
1. Check Node.js version compatibility
2. Install dependencies with `npm install`
3. Copy environment variables from `.env.example`
4. Run type checking with `npm run typecheck`

### During Development
1. Use TypeScript for type safety
2. Run linter frequently to catch issues early
3. Write tests for new features
4. Use meaningful commit messages
5. Review code changes before committing

### Before Committing
1. Run full test suite: `npm test`
2. Check linting: `npm run lint`
3. Verify formatting: `npm run format:check`
4. Run type checking: `npm run typecheck`
5. Test production build: `npm run build`

content.thinking:
The user wants to move the CLAUDE.md file to C:\Users\User\.claude directory. Let me check if that directory exists and what's in it.

Actually, Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md from specific locations. The `.claude` directory is indeed the Claude Code config directory. Let me check if it exists first, then move/copy the CLAUDE.md there.

Actually, Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md from the current working directory and parent directories. But putting it in `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` would make it a global instruction file that applies to ALL sessions regardless of working directory. That's actually a better location for global directives like this.


Let me verify the .claude directory exists and check its current contents.

content.text: Bonne idee. Le dossier `.claude` est le repertoire de configuration global de Claude Code, donc les instructions y seront lues dans **toutes** les sessions, peu importe le repertoire de travail.

- `npm test` or `npm run test` - Run all tests
- `npm run test:watch` - Run tests in watch mode
- `npm run test:coverage` - Run tests with coverage report
- `npm run test:unit` - Run unit tests only
- `npm run test:integration` - Run integration tests only
- `npm run test:e2e` - Run end-to-end tests

### Code Quality Commands
- `npm run lint` - Run ESLint for code linting
- `npm run lint:fix` - Run ESLint with auto-fix
- `npm run format` - Format code with Prettier
- `npm run format:check` - Check code formatting
- `npm run typecheck` - Run TypeScript type checking

### Development Tools
- `npm run storybook` - Start Storybook (if available)
- `npm run analyze` - Analyze bundle size
- `npm run clean` - Clean build artifacts

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies
- **JavaScript/TypeScript** - Primary programming languages
- **Node.js** - Runtime environment
- **npm/yarn** - Package management

### Common Frameworks
- **React** - UI library with hooks and functional components
- **Vue.js** - Progressive framework for building user interfaces
- **Angular** - Full-featured framework for web applications
- **Express.js** - Web application framework for Node.js
- **Next.js** - React framework with SSR/SSG capabilities

### Build Tools
- **Vite** - Fast build tool and development server
- **Webpack** - Module bundler
- **Rollup** - Module bundler for libraries
- **esbuild** - Extremely fast JavaScript bundler

### Testing Framework
- **Jest** - JavaScript testing framework
- **Vitest** - Fast unit test framework
- **Testing Library** - Simple and complete testing utilities
- **Cypress** - End-to-end testing framework
- **Playwright** - Cross-browser testing

### Code Quality Tools
- **ESLint** - JavaScript/TypeScript linter
- **Prettier** - Code formatter
- **TypeScript** - Static type checking
- **Husky** - Git hooks

## Project Structure Guidelines

### File Organization
```
src/
├── components/     # Reusable UI components
├── pages/         # Page components or routes
├── hooks/         # Custom React hooks
├── utils/         # Utility functions
├── services/      # API calls and external services
├── types/         # TypeScript type definitions
├── constants/     # Application constants
├── styles/        # Global styles and themes
└── tests/         # Test files
```

### Naming Conventions
- **Files**: Use kebab-case for file names (`user-profile.component.ts`)
- **Components**: Use PascalCase for component names (`UserProfile`)
- **Functions**: Use camelCase for function names (`getUserData`)
- **Constants**: Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants (`API_BASE_URL`)
- **Types/Interfaces**: Use PascalCase with descriptive names (`UserData`, `ApiResponse`)

## TypeScript Guidelines

### Type Safety
- Enable strict mode in `tsconfig.json`
- Use explicit types for function parameters and return values
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Use union types for multiple possible values
- Avoid `any` type - use `unknown` when type is truly unknown

### Best Practices
- Use type guards for runtime type checking
- Leverage utility types (`Partial`, `Pick`, `Omit`, etc.)
- Create custom types for domain-specific data
- Use enums for finite sets of values
- Document complex types with JSDoc comments

## Code Quality Standards

### ESLint Configuration
- Use recommended ESLint rules for JavaScript/TypeScript
- Enable React-specific rules if using React
- Configure import/export rules for consistent module usage
- Set up accessibility rules for inclusive development

### Prettier Configuration
- Use consistent indentation (2 spaces recommended)
- Set maximum line length (80-100 characters)
- Use single quotes for strings
- Add trailing commas for better git diffs

### Testing Standards
- Aim for 80%+ test coverage
- Write unit tests for utilities and business logic
- Use integration tests for component interactions
- Implement e2e tests for critical user flows
- Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)

## Performance Optimization

### Bundle Optimization
- Use code splitting for large applications
- Implement lazy loading for routes and components
- Optimize images and assets
- Use tree shaking to eliminate dead code
- Analyze bundle size regularly

### Runtime Performance
- Implement proper memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback)
- Use virtualization for large lists
- Optimize re-renders in React applications
- Implement proper error boundaries
- Use web workers for heavy computations

## Security Guidelines

### Dependencies
- Regularly audit dependencies with `npm audit`
- Keep dependencies updated
- Use lock files (`package-lock.json`, `yarn.lock`)
- Avoid dependencies with known vulnerabilities

### Code Security
- Sanitize user inputs
- Use HTTPS for API calls
- Implement proper authentication and authorization
- Store sensitive data securely (environment variables)
- Use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers

## Development Workflow

### Before Starting
1. Check Node.js version compatibility
2. Install dependencies with `npm install`
3. Copy environment variables from `.env.example`
4. Run type checking with `npm run typecheck`

### During Development
1. Use TypeScript for type safety
2. Run linter frequently to catch issues early
3. Write tests for new features
4. Use meaningful commit messages
5. Review code changes before committing

### Before Committing
1. Run full test suite: `npm test`
2. Check linting: `npm run lint`
3. Verify formatting: `npm run format:check`
4. Run type checking: `npm run typecheck`
5. Test production build: `npm run build`

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Directive de session obligatoire

**A CHAQUE NOUVELLE SESSION**, Claude Code DOIT :

1. **Consulter le fichier `~/claude-capabilities.md`** pour connaitre l'ensemble des skills, commandes GSD, agents et superpowers disponibles.
2. **Analyser la demande de l'utilisateur** et identifier quels skills, agents ou commandes GSD sont les plus adaptes pour y repondre.
3. **Utiliser proactivement les skills et agents pertinents** plutot que de tout faire manuellement. Par exemple :
   - Pour du debug → utiliser le skill `systematic-debugging` ou `debug`
   - Pour de l'architecture → utiliser `senior-architect` ou `Plan` agent
   - Pour des tests → utiliser `test`, `tdd-workflow`, ou le framework adapte au langage
   - Pour de la securite → utiliser `security-review` ou `owasp-security`
   - Pour la gestion de projet → utiliser les commandes `/gsd:*`
   - Pour de la recherche → utiliser l'agent `Explore` ou `deep-research`
   - Pour des taches paralleles → utiliser `dispatching-parallel-agents`
4. **Proposer les skills pertinents** a l'utilisateur quand une tache pourrait en beneficier, meme si l'utilisateur ne les mentionne pas explicitement.

> **Fichier de reference** : `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md`
> Ce fichier contient le catalogue complet des ~70 skills, ~25 commandes GSD, ~15 agents et les superpowers.

1→# ===============================
     2→# LeadRadar - Frontend (Vite)
     3→# ===============================
     4→VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.supabase.co
     5→VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
     6→VITE_APP_URL=http://localhost:5173
     7→
     8→# ===============================
     9→# LeadRadar - Supabase / Edge Functions
    10→# ===============================
    11→SUPABASE_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.supabase.co
    12→SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
    13→SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=YOUR_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
    14→SUPABASE_FUNCTIONS_URL=https://YOUR_PROJECT.functions.supabase.co
    15→
    16→# ===============================
    17→# Clés IA (SECRETS SUPABASE — JAMAIS côté client)
    18→# ===============================
    19→# Ces clés doivent être configurées via les secrets Supabase :
    20→#   supabase secrets set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
    21→#   supabase secrets set PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=pplx-...
    22→#   supabase secrets set GEMINI_API_KEY=AIza...
    23→#
    24→# NE PAS les mettre dans .env.local
    25→# NE PAS les préfixer par VITE_
    26→# Elles sont utilisées par l'edge function ai-proxy (server-side uniquement)
    27→GEMINI_API_KEY=YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY
    28→OPENAI_API_KEY=YOUR_OPENAI_API_KEY
    29→PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=YOUR_PERPLEXITY_API_KEY
    30→
    31→# ===============================
    32→# Stripe
    33→# ===============================
    34→STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_or_test_xxx
    35→STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_xxx
    36→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_20=price_xxx_credits20
    37→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_50=price_xxx_credits50
    38→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_120=price_xxx_credits120
    39→STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_300=price_xxx_credits300
    40→
    41→# ===============================
    42→# Email (Resend)
    43→# ===============================
    44→RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxx
    45→RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=LeadRadar <alerts@leadradar.io>
    46→
    47→# ===============================
    48→# App & URLs
    49→# ===============================
    50→APP_URL=http://localhost:5173
    51→DOMAIN=leadradar.io
    52→
    53→# ===============================
    54→# n8n Workflow Sources
    55→# ===============================
    56→N8N_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.example.com/webhook/leadradar
    57→N8N_WEBHOOK_TOKEN=optional_shared_secret
    58→N8N_LOG_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.example.com/webhook/leadradar-log
    59→PAP_API_URL=https://api.example.com/pap
    60→DVF_API_URL=https://api.gouv.fr/dvf
    61→LINKEDIN_SIGNAL_API_URL=https://api.linkedin.com/v2/search
    62→LINKEDIN_API_TOKEN=linkedin_token
    63→SITADEL2_API_URL=https://api.gouv.fr/sitadel2
    64→BOAMP_API_URL=https://api.boamp.fr/v1
    65→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_CITY=Paris
    66→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_ZIP=75000
    67→LEADRADAR_DEFAULT_REGION=Ile-de-France
    68→

content.content:
# Frase Sign-In Page UI/UX Analysis

## Layout Structure
The page employs a **centered card layout** with a full-screen gradient background. The main content area consists of a single authentication card positioned in the middle of the viewport, featuring a "flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center" container structure.

## Visual Design & Color Scheme
- **Background**: Gradient transitioning "from-surface-base to-surface-sunken"
- **Card styling**: "rounded-xl border bg-surface-elevated p-8 shadow-xl"
- **Typography**: Uses a system supporting multiple font families including Inter, JetBrains Mono, and Libre Baskerville

## Key UI Components

**Header Section**:
- Large heading: "Sign in to Frase"
- Subheading: "Enter your email to continue"
- Centered text alignment with "mb-6 text-center" styling

**Form Elements**:
- Email input field
- "Continue" button (primary action)
- Sign-up link: "Don't have an account? Sign up"

## Interactive Elements
- Primary call-to-action button for form submission
- Text link navigation to sign-up page
- Email input with validation support

## Design Philosophy
Minimalist, focused authentication flow emphasizing simplicity and clarity. The centered card approach reduces cognitive load, directing user attention exclusively toward credential entry.

# Frase Sign-In Page UI/UX Analysis

## Layout Structure
The page employs a **centered card layout** with a full-screen gradient background. The main content area consists of a single authentication card positioned in the middle of the viewport, featuring a "flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center" container structure.

## Visual Design & Color Scheme
- **Background**: Gradient transitioning "from-surface-base to-surface-sunken"
- **Card styling**: "rounded-xl border bg-surface-elevated p-8 shadow-xl"
- **Typography**: Uses a system supporting multiple font families including Inter, JetBrains Mono, and Libre Baskerville

## Key UI Components

**Header Section**:
- Large heading: "Sign in to Frase"
- Subheading: "Enter your email to continue"
- Centered text alignment with "mb-6 text-center" styling

**Form Elements**:
- Email input field
- "Continue" button (primary action)
- Sign-up link: "Don't have an account? Sign up"

## Interactive Elements
- Primary call-to-action button for form submission
- Text link navigation to sign-up page
- Email input with validation support

## Design Philosophy
Minimalist, focused authentication flow emphasizing simplicity and clarity. The centered card approach reduces cognitive load, directing user attention exclusively toward credential entry.

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**CitedBy.ai** is an AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) intelligence platform designed to optimize website visibility in AI-generated responses and answer engines. It's a modern web application that provides auditing, monitoring, and generative tools for SEO professionals and agencies.

**Current Status**: Version 2 (V2) Next.js backend + Version 3 (V3) Vite React frontend migration completed (2026-02-17)

**Location**: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai`

### 4. KEY FEATURES & FUNCTIONALITY

#### **A. Audit System (Core Business)**
- **URL Analysis** — Simulated scan (2.5s) generating:
  - 50 AEO criteria across multiple categories
  - Deterministic scoring (seed-based on URL)
  - Category breakdown with item-level details
  - Recommendations (critical/high/medium/low)
  
- **AI Citation Detection**
  - Probability scoring (0-100%)
  - Before/after citation comparison
  - 6 AI engines tracked (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, AI Overviews)

#### **B. Credit System (Monetization)**
- **Three Plan Levels**:
  - **Free**: 1 new project/month, 50 credits trial
  - **Pro**: 3 new projects/month, subscription billing
  - **Enterprise**: 1000+ projects/month (unlimited)

- **Credit Packs**:
  - Le Jerrican: 50 credits / €15
  - La Pompe: 200 credits / €49 (popular, +10% bonus)
  - La Citerne: 1000 credits / €199 (agency tier, +20% bonus)

- **Cost Structure** (defined in services):
  - Each tool operation deducts credits
  - Monthly renewal on subscription anniversary

#### **C. Generative Tools (6 Pre-generable)**
1. **Robots.txt Generator** — AI-optimized crawl rules
2. **Schema.org Creator** — JSON-LD structured data
3. **llms.txt Authoring** — LLM guidance document
4. **Answer-First Audit** — 6-category citability assessment
5. **E-E-A-T Builder** — Experience/Expertise/Authoritativeness/Trust scoring
6. **SMC Studio** — Semantic content creation

#### **D. Content Tools**
- **Blog Optimizer** — AI-powered article improvement
- **Content Writer** — B2B vertical content (9 verticals)
- **AI Citation Simulator** — Citation probability prediction
- **AI Response Layer** — Generate AI-optimized responses

#### **E. Technical Tools**
- **Directory** — Business listing index
- **Publisher Search/Request** — Outreach marketplace
- **Source of Truth** — Entity canonical data
- **AI Monitoring** — Citation tracking dashboard
- **Bot Tracking** — AI crawler detection
- **Reputation Scanner** — Brand sentiment in AI responses

#### **F. Account Management**
- **Profile Tab** — Name, email, API keys
- **Subscription Tab** — Plan selection, billing
- **Billing Tab** — Invoice history, payment methods
- **White Label** — Custom branding for agencies
- **Affiliation** — Partner program info

---

### 5. DATA MODELS & TYPES

#### **Core Entities** (from `types.ts`)

```typescript
// User Account
User {
  id: string

  credits: number

// Audit Result (Core Data)
AnalysisResult {
  url: string

  globalScore: number (0-100)

  recommendations: Recommendation[]

    before: string
    after: string
    probability: number
  }
  robotsTxtData?: RobotsTxtData
  generatedSolutions?: Record<string, GeneratedSolution>
  preGenerated?: PreGeneratedSolutions
}

// Category Score
CategoryScore {
  name: string
  score: number
  weight: number
  items: Array<{
    status: 'success' | 'warning' | 'error'
    message: string
    recommendation?: string
  }>
}

// Recommendation
Recommendation {
  id: string
  priority: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'
  title: string
  description: string
  action: string
  effort: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
  impact: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'

}

// Generated Solution
GeneratedSolution {
  title: string

---

### 11. KEY BUSINESS LOGIC

#### **Audit Scoring Algorithm**
1. Parse URL → extract hostname
2. Evaluate 50 criteria (seed-based determinism)
3. Group by 5 categories (SEO, Technical, Content, Entity, Performance)
4. Calculate category scores (% passed)
5. Global score = average of categories
6. Generate recommendations for failures

#### **Credit Deduction** (in App.tsx)
```typescript
handleDeductCredits(amount) {
  if (user.credits < amount) {
    alert("Insufficient credits");
    navigate('recharge');
    return false;
  }
  user.credits -= amount;
  return true;
}
```

#### **Monthly Project Limit** (Usage Tracking)
- Count unique hostnames in audit history
- Filter by current billing cycle (1st of month)
- Enforce plan limits:
  - Free: 1 project/month
  - Pro: 3 projects/month
  - Enterprise: 1000+

#### **Pre-Generation (Background)**
- Triggered after audit: `megaGenerate(url)`
- Generates 6 tools in parallel via Gemini
- Cached in `AnalysisResult.preGenerated`
- Status: idle → generating → completed/failed
- UI shows badges (Generating / Ready / None)

#### **AI Citation Simulation** (In `answer-first-audit.ts`)
- 6 categories: Content Freshness, FAQ Structure, Semantic HTML, Schema.org, Meta Optimization, Answer-First Format
- Score: 0-100 based on heuristics
- Probability: weighted by content structure
- E-E-A-T pillar mapping: Experience/Expertise/Authority/Trust

#### **API Keys Management**

- Frontend never sees secrets
- API_KEY for Gemini (placeholder in .env.local)

#### **Database Security**
- Row-level security (RLS) policies
- Service role for admin actions
- Anon key for public operations
- Encrypted password storage (Supabase Auth)

## SUMMARY

**CitedBy.ai** is a sophisticated AEO platform combining:
1. **Audit Engine** — Deterministic scoring of 50 SEO criteria
2. **AI Integration** — Google Gemini for content generation
3. **Monetization** — Dual model (subscriptions + credit packs)

### 11. KEY BUSINESS LOGIC

#### **Audit Scoring Algorithm**
1. Parse URL → extract hostname
2. Evaluate 50 criteria (seed-based determinism)
3. Group by 5 categories (SEO, Technical, Content, Entity, Performance)
4. Calculate category scores (% passed)
5. Global score = average of categories
6. Generate recommendations for failures

#### **Credit Deduction** (in App.tsx)
```typescript
handleDeductCredits(amount) {
  if (user.credits < amount) {
    alert("Insufficient credits");
    navigate('recharge');
    return false;
  }
  user.credits -= amount;
  return true;
}
```

#### **Monthly Project Limit** (Usage Tracking)
- Count unique hostnames in audit history
- Filter by current billing cycle (1st of month)
- Enforce plan limits:
  - Free: 1 project/month
  - Pro: 3 projects/month
  - Enterprise: 1000+

#### **Pre-Generation (Background)**
- Triggered after audit: `megaGenerate(url)`
- Generates 6 tools in parallel via Gemini
- Cached in `AnalysisResult.preGenerated`
- Status: idle → generating → completed/failed
- UI shows badges (Generating / Ready / None)

#### **AI Citation Simulation** (In `answer-first-audit.ts`)
- 6 categories: Content Freshness, FAQ Structure, Semantic HTML, Schema.org, Meta Optimization, Answer-First Format
- Score: 0-100 based on heuristics
- Probability: weighted by content structure
- E-E-A-T pillar mapping: Experience/Expertise/Authority/Trust

---

### 12. CRITICAL FILES BY FUNCTIONALITY

| Feature | Files |
|---------|-------|
| Routing | App.tsx |
| Audit Engine | services/analyzer.ts, services/criteria.ts |
| Generative Tools | services/mega-generator.ts, services/generator.ts |
| E-E-A-T Scoring | services/eeat/* (8 files) |
| Answer-First | services/answer-first-audit.ts |
| Content Generation | services/content_writer.ts, services/smc.ts |
| Reputation Scanning | services/reputation_service.ts |
| Payment | components/StripeCheckout.tsx, lib/stripe.ts |
| Auth | pages/Login.tsx, lib/supabase/* |
| Persistence | services/database.ts (localStorage) |
| Design | globals.css, tailwind.config.ts |
| AI Provider | lib/ai-provider.ts, lib/ai-proxy.ts |

---

### 13. BUILD & DEPLOYMENT

#### **Build Process**
```bash
npm run build  # Vite build (2451 modules, ~23s)
└─ Output: dist/ (production bundle)
```

#### **Development**
```bash
npm run dev    # Vite dev server (port 5173, hot reload)
```

#### **Testing**
```bash
npm test       # Vitest unit tests
npm run test:coverage  # Coverage report
```

#### **Deployment** (Vercel)
- `vercel.json` — Configuration
- Edge functions → Supabase
- Frontend → Vercel CDN
- Database → Supabase PostgreSQL

---

### 14. SECURITY & CONFIGURATION

#### **Headers** (next.config.mjs)
- X-Frame-Options: DENY (clickjacking protection)
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Permissions-Policy: camera, microphone, geolocation blocked

#### **API Keys Management**
- Stored in Supabase secrets (not .env.local)
- Edge functions access via environment
- Frontend never sees secrets
- API_KEY for Gemini (placeholder in .env.local)

#### **Database Security**
- Row-level security (RLS) policies
- Service role for admin actions
- Anon key for public operations
- Encrypted password storage (Supabase Auth)

---

### 15. KNOWN ISSUES & TODOs

#### **Stripe Integration**
- [ ] Replace mock card form with `@stripe/react-stripe-js`
- [ ] Connect real Stripe account
- [ ] Test webhook handling
- [ ] Enable subscription management

#### **Supabase Backend**
- [ ] Migrate user auth from mock to Supabase
- [ ] Implement PostgreSQL schema migrations
- [ ] Edge function deployment
- [ ] Real-time sync via Supabase subscriptions

#### **Feature Completeness**
- Placeholder pages exist (Tracked Queries, Benchmark, My Orders, etc.)
- Some API endpoints stubbed
- Content Writer vertical selection (9 verticals defined)

#### **Performance**
- LocalStorage limit (~5-10MB) for audit history
- Large PDF exports may need optimization
- Mega-generation (6 tools) runs sequentially

---

### 16. TESTING COVERAGE

Located in `tests/`:
- **database.test.ts** — LocalStorage persistence tests
- **scoring.test.ts** — Score calculation validation
- Framework: Vitest 4.0 + jsdom
- Coverage target: 80%+

---

### 17. DOCUMENTATION

- **CLAUDE.md** — Comprehensive project instructions (8.8KB)
- **Config files** — Well-commented (CSS variables, type definitions)
- **Service modules** — Function-level JSDoc
- **n8n workflows** — Business process automation
- **qa/criteria.json** — 50 audit criteria definitions

---

## SUMMARY

**CitedBy.ai** is a sophisticated AEO platform combining:
1. **Audit Engine** — Deterministic scoring of 50 SEO criteria
2. **AI Integration** — Google Gemini for content generation
3. **Monetization** — Dual model (subscriptions + credit packs)
4. **Marketplace** — Directory & publisher outreach
5. **White Label** — Agency branding support

**Tech Excellence**: Modern React SPA (Vite), TypeScript, Tailwind, responsive design, mock auth/payments ready for production integration.

**Current State**: V3 frontend migration complete, V2 backend intact, ready for Supabase & Stripe integration.

## Frase.io Dashboard Overview

**Interface Design:**
The interface of Frase is straightforward, and Frase got a new, simpler look at the beginning of 2024 after the 2.0 update rolled out.

**Dashboard Layout:**
After you sign up for a free Frase account, you'll end up in a dashboard with three buttons to create a new document. When you first log in to Frase, you'll be brought to your dashboard area, and from the side navigation bar, you can access all the AI and SEO tools you need.

**Document Creation Options:**
Users can choose from SEO Workflow (inserting a keyword for Frase to analyze top search results), or Blank Document (starting with an empty page to write using AI tools first).

**Key Interface Features:**
After the analysis is done, you'll see 4 tabs to the right of the editor window, including the SERP tab which shows the "People Also Ask" section at the top and a list of the top currently ranking results, with Frase pulling the first 2 pages from Google SERPS (normally 20 results).

There is a percentage value in the top right corner that shows how optimized your article is compared to competition.

The search results contain multiple 2025-2026 reviews with interface descriptions, though they don't include direct screenshot galleries of the current dashboard.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of Frase.io's SEO tool features, layout, sidebar navigation, and content editor:

## Platform Layout & Navigation

Frase has a super clean main dashboard with bare minimal navigation elements, featuring a very minimalistic user interface that is easy to navigate. The workspace is uncluttered for writing, and all the tools and options you need are neatly tucked into the sidebars, making them easy to find.

From the side navigation bar, you can access all the AI and SEO tools you need, and the menu is straightforward, allowing you to jump between research, writing, and SEO tools with just a couple of clicks.

## Content Editor Features

Frase's content editing software stands out for its ability to conduct SERP-based research directly within the document and its intuitive user interface, with powerful AI directives just a click away. Users get a distraction-free interface to build content, and can trigger the AI writer by clicking "Write for Me" at any point in the editor.

## Sidebar Elements

The right side of the menu shows all the elements of the content editor including SERP, Brief, Optimize, Questions, AI writer, and Templates. You can click the Optimize tab from the right-hand sidebar to see a list of keywords related to your main target keyword.

## Key SEO Features

Frase provides real-time scoring for both SEO and GEO in one editor. The SEO tools help you discover keywords, optimize your content, and rank higher in the SERPs, while Frase has a powerful and easy-to-use outline tool where you can look through the top 20 results and easily pull in headers for your article.

content.content:
# Frase.io UI/UX Interface Details

## Dashboard Layout
The dashboard features a side navigation bar on the left with access to AI and SEO tools. Users manage projects from a **Documents** page where they can organize work into folders.

## Navigation Structure
Main navigation includes:
- Documents (project management)
- AI Writer tools
- SEO optimization features
- Chatbot builder (Frase Answers)
- Analytics/insights section

The interface uses tab-based navigation within the content editor.

## Color Scheme
The design employs a clean, professional aesthetic with white backgrounds and dark text for the main interface. Accent colors include blue for interactive elements and highlights.

## Content Editor Layout
The editor mirrors "Google Docs or Microsoft Word" functionality with:
- **Main window**: Text writing and formatting via top toolbar
- **Right sidebar**: AI workflow tools organized in tabs
- **Toolbar options**: Image insertion, text formatting, styling controls

## Right Sidebar Components
The Research tab displays:
- Competitor article analysis from top 20 search results
- Header suggestions with insertion options
- Target word count recommendations
- Suggested image, header, and link quantities
- Content brief generator ("Automate Content Brief")
- SERP metrics visualizer

## AI-Powered Editing Tools
When text is highlighted, a contextual toolbar appears with options including:
- Expand (with "Write about this" and "Insert Transition" options)
- Rewrite functionality (sentence-by-sentence or paragraph-by-paragraph)
- Additional editing commands

## Document Management
The Documents page allows creation of new articles or importing existing content. Users select target keywords, language preferences, and folder organization before opening the Content Editor.

## Design Philosophy
The interface prioritizes workflow efficiency with integrated research, writing, and optimization tools accessible simultaneously, reducing context-switching between applications.

content.content:
# Frase.io Product Summary

## Core Platform Description
Frase positions itself as "The Agentic SEO & GEO Platform" designed to research, write, optimize, and track content across both traditional and AI search engines.

## Key Features & Capabilities

**Primary Features:**
- AI Agent with 80+ specialized skills
- SEO content optimization with real-time scoring
- GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) optimization
- AI search tracking across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and Google AI
- Content opportunity identification
- SERP research and competitive analysis
- Content atomization (repurposing into multiple formats)
- Programmatic SEO for scale
- Brand governance tools
- Content clustering for topical authority

## Workflow Sections

The platform emphasizes a four-step workflow:
1. **Analyze** - Studies top 10 competitors, identifies gaps (30 seconds)
2. **Create & Optimize** - Real-time scoring during writing
3. **Track** - Monitors Google and AI platform visibility
4. **Grow** - Surfaces prioritized opportunities

## Visual Interface Elements

**Dashboard Components:**
- Content score display (0-100 scale)
- SEO and GEO scores shown separately
- AI visibility dashboard showing share of voice across platforms with progress bars
- Competitor analysis cards showing topic counts
- Real-time metrics: citations, AI crawler activity
- Color-coded status indicators (green for positive trends)

**Color Scheme:**
- Primary: Forest green (#059469)
- Secondary: Cream/beige background (#FAF5F0 approx.)
- Accent: Teal, gold, forest variations
- Text: Dark ink gray for primary, lighter grays for secondary

**Navigation:**
Top-level menu includes: Product (with sub-items), Solutions, Pricing, Resources, Blog, and authentication options.

## AI Visibility Metrics Tracking

The platform displays real-time metrics:
- ChatGPT: 78%
- Perplexity: 65%
- Claude: 72%
- Gemini: 58%
- Google AI: 85%

Each with visual progress bars and percentage displays.

## Pricing

**Starting Plan:** $39/month (Starter) with full access to AI Agent, content optimization, and SERP research

**Trial Offering:** 7-day free trial, no credit card required

## Key Taglines & Value Propositions

- "Rank on Google. Get Cited by AI."
- Described as "content marketing cheat codes" (customer quote)
- Emphasizes doing the work rather than adding dashboards
- Full workflow in one workspace vs. five separate tools

## Customer Base
"Trusted by thousands of content & marketing teams" including ActiveCampaign, Coursera, Microsoft, Oracle, and others (20+ logos displayed).

## Unique Positioning
Distinguishes itself by handling the complete workflow—research through tracking—rather than single-task tools, with particular emphasis on optimization for AI-powered search engines alongside traditional SEO.

content.content:
# Frase.io Dashboard UI and Visual Design

## Dashboard Overview
When users first log in, they're directed to a dashboard area with a side navigation bar providing access to all AI and SEO tools. The interface emphasizes project management through a dedicated **Documents** page.

## Content Editor Interface
The Content Editor features what the review describes as "one of the best UIs we've seen." The layout includes:

- **Main writing window**: Functions similarly to Google Docs or Microsoft Word with formatting toolbar at the top
- **Right-side panel**: Contains AI-powered workflow tools organized into tabs
- **Research tab**: Displays competitor analysis data including headers from top-ranking articles
- **Toolbar interactions**: When text is highlighted, contextual AI options appear

## Key Visual Elements

The interface provides:
- Organized sidebar navigation for tool access
- Visual SERP analysis showing competitor metrics like "word count, topic score, domain rating, headings count"
- Folder organization system for projects
- Pop-up toolbars with AI editing options (Expand, Rewrite, etc.)

## Layout Design Philosophy
The design prioritizes workflow efficiency by placing research data and AI tools alongside the main editor, allowing users to reference optimization recommendations while writing without switching windows.

content.content:
# Frase SEO Research Page Layout Analysis

## Dashboard Layout
The page presents a feature-focused design with a hero section that immediately communicates the core value proposition. The main interface screenshot shows "Research, analyze, and outline all in one interface," demonstrating an integrated workspace combining SERP analysis, question discovery, and outline generation.

## UI Components
The dashboard includes several key elements:
- **SERP Analysis section** displaying top 10 ranking articles with word count and domain authority metrics
- **Query Research panel** surfacing "People Also Ask questions" and related search terms
- **Auto-Generated Outlines** with AI-suggested headings and customizable structure
- Card-based layouts for feature presentation with icons and descriptive text

## Navigation Structure
The site header contains:
- Primary navigation linking to Platform Overview, AI Agent, AI Search Tracking, and Content Optimization
- Solutions menu (Content Marketers, SEO Teams, Agencies, Enterprises)
- Resources section with Blog, SEO + GEO Guide, and Free SEO Tools
- Call-to-action buttons for "Start Free Trial" and account login

## Color Scheme
- **Primary accent**: Forest green ("var(--forest-500)"), used for buttons, icons, and highlights
- **Backgrounds**: Cream tones ("var(--cream-50/300)") for sections and cards
- **Text**: Dark ink shades ("var(--ink-900/500)") for hierarchy
- Modern, clean aesthetic with rounded corners and subtle shadows

## Content Editor Integration
Users can "Export your outline to Frase's editor and start creating optimized content" with research remaining accessible in the sidebar during writing.

## Frase.io Dashboard Overview

Frase got a new, simpler look at the beginning of 2024 after the 2.0 update rolled out, with most older reviews showing outdated interfaces.

### Current Dashboard Features:

When you first log in to Frase, you'll be brought to your dashboard area, from which you can access all the AI and SEO tools you need via the side navigation bar, and manage your projects from the Documents page.

After signing up for a free Frase account, you'll end up in a dashboard with three buttons to create a new document.

### Key Dashboard Elements:

After analysis is done, you'll see 4 tabs to the right of the editor window, with the SERP tab showing the "People Also Ask" section at the top and a list of the top currently ranking results, pulling the first 2 pages from Google (normally 20 results).

Users particularly like the dashboard that shows how their article stacks up to others competing for that keyword, making it easy to understand what needs to be added or subtracted to rank higher.

The search results don't provide direct links to actual dashboard screenshots from 2025-2026, but the reviews and product pages describe the interface as straightforward and intuitive with recent updates.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Frase.io's content editor interface layout and sidebar tabs:

## Sidebar Tabs Overview

There are 5 main tabs on the right-side panel in Frase, which include:

1. **Research Tab**: Frase analyzes the top 20 SERP results (Google results) for your search query and provides average word, header, link and image counts. This section also shows each of the top results, returning them as an outline with clickable content that is instantly added to the editor and cited with a link to the source.

2. **Outline Tab**: Click the headers from the SERP results in a horizontally scrollable view to add them to a list (with drag-and-drop order ability) that can be pasted into the editor as an outline.

3. **Tools Tab**: With the "In Editor" tab, you can set Background Context for the AI writer, include optimization topics, and set the tone of the "write" button in the editor. The "Tools" and "Community" section will provide AI writing tools to use (like a blog intro tool, product description tool ect.), and in the "Chat" section, you can write a command to spur an AI output.

4. **Optimize Tab**: Frase scans the top 20 search results for your query (or whichever sources you include in the list) and automatically extracts their key topics.

## Interface Design

All the tools and options you need are neatly tucked into the sidebars, making them easy to find when you need them. When it comes to writing, the workspace is uncluttered, letting you focus on the content without any distractions.

# Frase.io Dashboard Interface Overview

## Layout & Navigation

The Frase.io dashboard features an **intuitive design** where users can "intuitively guess what every feature inside the dashboard is used for without reading tutorials."

## Key UI Components

**Document Creation:**
The platform allows users to create new documents for content projects. When starting a project, you select a search type (such as "Top Google results") and initiate document creation.

**Content Structure Analysis:**
Once a document is created, "Frase.io analyzes top-ranking results and shows their content structure with H1, H2, and H3 headlines." Users can then scroll through available options and select specific paragraphs to include.

**Optimization Dashboard:**
The optimization interface displays keywords highlighted on the right side, showing "the number of times those keywords were included in the article" and comparing optimization metrics against top competitors.

## Visual Components

The review includes several screenshots demonstrating:
- The main dashboard view
- Document creation workflow
- Title generator interface
- Optimization tools panel

The author notes the interface is user-friendly enough that "anyone might" navigate features without extensive tutorials, though the optimization recommendations require SEO expertise to interpret correctly.

## Dashboard Layout

The dashboard features a **straightforward, left-sidebar navigation system**. When users first log in, they land on the "Frase feed" where they can access core functionality.

## Main Navigation Structure

The left sidebar provides access to different modules, including:
- Question Ideas
- Concept Map
- Workflow options
- Content creation tools

## Editor Layout

The editor interface divides the screen into sections:
- **Left area**: Main editing workspace
- **Right panel**: "Content brief" display showing competitor research data and optimization metrics

The review notes: "On the right-hand side of the screen, you will see the content brief" with options to paste content sections directly.

## Key UI Elements

Users interact with buttons throughout the interface—"Get Started," "Create Document," and "export" buttons guide the workflow. The tool displays a "topic score from 0 to 100%" to indicate SEO optimization levels.

## Overall Assessment

The reviewer characterizes the interface as **"straightforward"** and easy to navigate, though specific screenshots aren't included in this review.
